title: Peucedanum nagpurense

This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, many of which are aromatic herbs or shrubs. The genus Peucedanum is a group of plants that are primarily native to the Northern Hemisphere and are often found in temperate or alpine regions. Peucedanum nagpurense is one of the many species within this genus, with the specific classification being: Peucedanum nagpurense (Benth.) M.F.Raizada. The classification of Peucedanum nagpurense reflects its relationship to other plants in the Apiaceae family and its evolutionary history.
